title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description:
Given a rotated sorted array of distinct integers and a target, return its index if found, otherwise -1. The array was originally sorted in ascending order and rotated at some pivot unknown to you.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ len(nums) ≤ 10^5
- -10^9 ≤ nums[i], target ≤ 10^9
explanation:
Intuition
At any point at least one side around mid is sorted. Compare target to the sorted side to decide which half to discard. This preserves the logarithmic time guarantee.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Maintain left and right inclusive.
2. Main Algorithm: While left ≤ right, probe mid and identify the sorted half, then decide which half to keep.
3. Return Result: Return index when found or -1 otherwise.

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(log n) because the search space halves at each step.
- Space Complexity: O(1) since only a few variables are maintained.

Key Insights
- In a rotation, exactly one side is sorted unless equal elements exist
- Narrow the interval based on which side contains the target

Common Pitfalls
- Misidentifying the sorted side causes missed targets
- Not using ≤ and < carefully can loop forever
hints: One side of the split is always sorted
Check if target lies within that side's range
